name: Stakeholder Update Drafter
description: Draft a professional project status email for a specific stakeholder — including difficult messages like delays, budget overruns, or scope changes — with the right tone, framing, and a clear ask.
author: accilium

variables.name: PROJECT_CONTEXT
variables.description: Current project situation: progress, issues, decisions needed (bullet points are fine).
variables.example: Rollout phase 2 delayed by 3 weeks due to vendor API issues; budget still on track; need steering committee decision on scope of phase 3

variables.name: RECIPIENT
variables.description: Who receives the email and their relationship to the project.
variables.example: Program sponsor (client CIO), skeptical about timeline, hates surprises

variables.name: KEY_MESSAGES
variables.description: The 1-3 messages that must land, in order of importance.
variables.example: 1) Delay is contained and does not affect go-live; 2) We need the scope decision by Friday

variables.name: TONE
variables.example: Confident and transparent, no sugarcoating, no alarmism

example_input: PROJECT_CONTEXT: phase 2 delayed 3 weeks; RECIPIENT: client CIO; KEY_MESSAGES: delay contained, decision needed Friday; TONE: confident, transparent
